data.text: I found an existing `lint_hook.go`, so I’m checking whether it already satisfies the bead or needs tightening. Next I’m reading the hook, the quality hook types, and the tests around dispatch behavior so I can align the implementation with the exact contract.

The hook file is already present, so I’m verifying two things now: whether it already meets the contract exactly, and whether any gaps are in wiring or tests rather than the hook body itself. After that I’ll run the scoped Go tests to see if the repo is already green or if there’s a narrow fix left.

    32	func newLintHookTestRoot(t *testing.T) string {
    33		t.Helper()
    34	
    35		root := t.TempDir()
    36		skillPath := filepath.Join(root, ".agents", "skills", "ddx", "bead-lifecycle")
    37		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(skillPath, 0o755))

    41

    43		t.Helper()
    44	
    45		store := bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(root, ".ddx"))
    46		require.NoError(t, store.Init())
    47	
    48		dep := &bead.Bead{

    55		require.NoError(t, store.Create(dep))

    61			Status:    bead.StatusOpen,
    62			Priority:  2,

    64			Parent:    "ddx-parent",

    69			Acceptance:  "1. TestLintHook_UsesRunnerLibrary\n2. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... green\n3. lefthook run pre-commit passes",

    85	func TestLintHook_UsesRunnerLibrary(t *testing.T) {

   754						"threshold_score":  lintThreshold,

   761						"waivers_applied":  lintResult.WaiversApplied,
   762					})

   764				}
   765			}

   767			if err := w.Store.Claim(candidate.ID, assignee); err != nil {
